leads.summary: Taylor ISD's $147M November 2025 bond (Prop A, passed 59.61%) itemizes an artificial turf line item for baseball, softball, and other district athletic sites under 1,000-seat capacity; no per-project dollar amount or installation timeline is broken out.
leads.evidence_quote: Surface Improvements - Artificial Turf - Baseball, Softball & Other Sites with less than 1,000 seat capacity

evidence.project_name: Taylor ISD Prop A - Baseball/Softball Turf
evidence.details: Taylor ISD's $147 million Proposition A bond, approved by voters in November 2025 (59.61% in favor), includes a bond-document line item titled 'Surface Improvements - Artificial Turf - Baseball, Softball & Other Sites with less than 1,000 seat capacity,' stated purpose 'to address usability and durability.' District-wide, multi-site item covering baseball and softball fields plus unspecified other sites below 1,000-seat capacity. No individual dollar allocation, specific site list, or construction/start timeline given.

leads.summary: Williamson County Commissioners Court approved a sponsorship agreement with Lonestar Soccer Club to convert four grass fields to FIFA-certified synthetic turf at Southwest Williamson County Regional Park in Leander; total project cost ~$8.9M, approved Jan 14, 2026.
leads.evidence_quote: The conversion from grass to FIFA-certified synthetic turf will reduce the cost of maintenance for the fields, improve field quality and significantly increase available hours for use.

evidence.project_name: Southwest Williamson County Regional Park - Synthetic Turf Conversion
evidence.details: Press release dated January 14, 2026: Williamson County Commissioners Court approved a sponsorship agreement with Lonestar Soccer Club to convert four existing grass fields to FIFA-certified synthetic turf at Southwest Williamson County Regional Park. Supports more than 12,000 annual activities. Williamson County funds one field replacement through its Capital Improvement Program; Lonestar Soccer Club invests $6.5M for remaining three fields. Total estimated cost $8,925,020. Agreement term 8-20 years. No construction start date or bid/RFP details given.

leads.summary: New 150-acre, $219M Highland Lakes High School (HS2) campus opening Aug. 2029 will include a football stadium, baseball field, softball field, and a combined track & field/soccer stadium; CMAR selected March 2025, $46.36M Phase 1 GMP approved Sept. 2025, site work began Jan. 26, 2026, building construction starts May 11, 2026.
leads.evidence_quote: Football Stadium, Baseball Field, Softball Field, Track & Field/Soccer Stadium

evidence.project_name: Highland Lakes High School - New Stadium & Ballfields
evidence.project_address: Reimers-Peacock Road off SH 71, Austin, TX 78738
evidence.details: New 150-acre, $219M second high school campus (HS2). Football Stadium, Baseball Field, Softball Field, Track & Field/Soccer Stadium, plus competition gym, practice fields, 8 tennis courts. American Constructors selected CMAR March 2025; $46.36M Phase 1 GMP approved Sept 2025. Site work 2026-01-26 to 2029-03-13; building construction 2026-05-11 to 2029-02-20; opening Aug 2029. Page does not specify turf type/synthetic surfacing for any facility -- surface material not yet confirmed.

leads.summary: Austin ISD's 2022 bond funds a ~$25M renovation of Nelson Field, converting the baseball field to artificial turf plus grandstand, field house, press box, restroom and concession upgrades; design began fall 2023.
leads.evidence_quote: The project will also upgrade the baseball field to artificial turf and add new seating and lighting.

evidence.details: Part of Austin ISD's 2022 Bond Program (Central Athletic Facilities). Budget $25,000,000. Scope: grandstand renovations, field house updates, press box enhancements, restroom improvements, concession upgrades, converting baseball field to artificial turf with new seating and lighting. Project team: Vanir STV (PM); Stantec/Cadence McShane (Architecture & Construction). Design began fall 2023; construction timeline not specified.

leads.summary: Austin ISD's 2022 bond funds a ~$6.99M renovation of Yellow Jacket Stadium (football/soccer), installing artificial turf plus a new press box, fieldhouse, scoreboard, track resurfacing, lighting, seating and concessions; design began fall 2023.
leads.evidence_quote: Upgrades include artificial turf, press box, fieldhouse, scoreboard, resurfacing the track, lights, seating, and concessions.

evidence.details: Part of Austin ISD's 2022 Bond Program (Central Athletic Facilities). Budget $6,987,758. Serves football and soccer. Scope: artificial turf installation, press box, fieldhouse renovation, scoreboard upgrade, track resurfacing, lighting, seating, concessions upgrades. Project team: Vanir STV (PM); Fuse Architecture Studio (A&E). Design began fall 2023.

leads.summary: Austin ISD's 2022 bond funds a ~$4.77M upgrade of the Burger Sports Complex baseball field to artificial turf, with new lighting and seating; design began fall 2023.
leads.evidence_quote: Upgrades include artificial turf, lights, and seating for the baseball field.

evidence.project_name: Burger Sports Complex - Baseball Turf Upgrade
evidence.details: Part of Austin ISD's 2022 Bond Program (Central Athletic Facilities). Budget $4,767,000. Scope: converting baseball field to artificial turf, plus new lighting and seating. Design began fall 2023.

leads.summary: Austin ISD's 2022 bond funds a ~$2.67M upgrade of the Noack Sports Complex softball field to artificial turf, with new lighting and seating; design began fall 2023.
leads.evidence_quote: Upgrades include artificial turf, lights and seating for the softball field.

evidence.project_name: Noack Sports Complex - Softball Turf Upgrade
evidence.details: Part of Austin ISD's 2022 Bond Program (Central Athletic Facilities). Budget $2,667,000. Scope: converting softball field to artificial turf, plus new lighting and seating. Design began fall 2023.

leads.summary: City of Austin (Parks and Recreation Dept.) IFB 8600 MAS1009, 'Athletic Fields Maintenance and Renovation,' covers baseball/softball, soccer, and multi-purpose fields at designated recreation centers citywide; bids due July 21, 2026 at 2:00 PM. Published summary does not explicitly confirm artificial/synthetic turf scope -- flagged for review.
leads.evidence_quote: Maintenance of sports fields (baseball/softball, soccer, multi-purpose) at designated City recreation centers

evidence.project_name: Austin Rec Centers - Field Maintenance & Renovation
evidence.details: IFB 8600 MAS1009, Athletic Fields Maintenance and Renovation, issued by Austin Parks and Recreation Department. Scope: maintenance of sports fields (baseball/softball, soccer, multi-purpose) at designated City recreation centers; specific sites in a separate Field Locations attachment not parseable during this review. Issue date June 15, 2026; pre-bid meeting June 23, 2026; response due July 21, 2026 2:00 PM. Budget not published. Listing does not explicitly mention artificial/synthetic turf -- scope may be limited to natural-grass maintenance; should be confirmed against full bid package before BDM outreach.

leads.summary: Hays County's $2M Parks and Open Space Bond grant funds renovation of the North Hays Optimist (NHO) Youth Sportsplex in Kyle, a Pop Warner league complex for football, baseball, softball and cheer (ages 3-15); the source page does not state the surface type.
leads.evidence_quote: The project received a $2M commitment from the Parks Bond to enhance recreational opportunities for youth sports programs and improve facility restoration.

evidence.details: North Hays Optimist (NHO), only Pop Warner League in Hays County, serves ages 3-15 with football, baseball, softball, cheer programs. Hays Parks Bond project page confirms $2M commitment from county's Parks and Open Space Bond (2020 Prop A, $75M total). Commissioners Court minutes Feb 11 2025 corroborate active/funded status: poor field conditions noted, grant awarded to NHO, dispute ongoing over field management. No turf, synthetic surface, or construction scope/timeline stated; surface type unconfirmed, should be verified directly with Hays County Parks or NHO.

leads.summary: Round Rock's 2023 GO Bond-funded Athletic Performance Center at Old Settlers Park will add four multipurpose fields (plus an indoor gym and outdoor track); surface type is not specified on the city page, and status/timeline conflicts with later news reporting. Project is bond-funded and under design.
leads.evidence_quote: The City will build an Athletic Performance Center that will include an indoor gym, outdoor track and four multipurpose fields.

evidence.project_name: Athletic Performance Center - Four Multipurpose Fields
evidence.project_address: 3300 E. Palm Valley Blvd, Round Rock, TX 78665 (Old Settlers Park)
evidence.details: Part of City of Round Rock's $274M 2023 GO Bond program, under Old Settlers Park Expansion umbrella. Athletic Performance Center includes indoor gym, outdoor track, four multipurpose fields, plus parking and relocated Parks & Recreation admin offices. Official page lists status 'under design,' construction planned summer 2025 to fall 2026. Jan 2026 Community Impact report states construction already began summer 2025, completion now expected spring 2027 -- city page and news report disagree on status/date. Neither source specifies whether the four multipurpose fields will be synthetic turf, natural grass, or a mix -- unconfirmed, needs direct confirmation with city contact.

leads.summary: Round Rock is expanding the 60-acre Multipurpose Complex at Old Settlers Park with six new fields and collegiate-level press boxes; news reports describe five synthetic turf fields plus one natural grass field, ~$49M contract approved fall 2025, targeting 2027 completion under the 2023 GO Bond. City page lists status as 'under design'.
leads.evidence_quote: This project will construct six additional fields and collegiate level press boxes to provide more space for local team practices and draw additional tournaments for sports tourism.

evidence.project_name: Multipurpose Complex Expansion - Six New Fields
evidence.project_address: 3300 E. Palm Valley Blvd, Round Rock, TX 78665 (Old Settlers Park)
evidence.details: Part of City of Round Rock's $274M 2023 GO Bond program, under Old Settlers Park Expansion umbrella. Existing 60-acre Multipurpose Complex opened 2017 (10 fields); ~75% local team use, 25% sports-tourism. Expansion constructs six additional fields and collegiate-level press boxes. Existing tennis complex relocating separately (excluded, not target-sport turf item). Official city page lists status 'under design' with no completion date. Independent reporting (Community Impact, SportsVenue Technology) describes five synthetic turf fields and one natural grass championship field, ~$49M construction contract approved fall 2025, completion targeted 2027, resulting 27 fields within a quarter mile. These specifics not on official city page -- should be verified directly with city before quoting to a customer.

leads.summary: Leander ISD's turf replacement at A.C. Bible Jr. Memorial Stadium (football/soccer) is part of a $1.6M Bond 2023 savings reallocation shared with Monroe Stadium, approved by the Board Jan. 22, 2026; installation is reported underway as of the district's May 2026 status update (inferred from the shared project line item).
leads.evidence_quote: The Board approved this recommendation brought forward by the Bond Oversight Committee after reviewing the condition and remaining lifespan of the existing turf, which has reached the end of its usable life and presents growing safety concerns for student-athletes.

evidence.project_name: Bible Stadium - turf replacement, football/soccer field
evidence.projected_start_date: Summer 2026 (commencing later in May 2026, after Monroe Stadium, per shared bond line item)
evidence.details: Leander ISD Board (Jan. 22, 2026) approved reallocating $1.6 million in savings from the 2023 bond's high-school baseball/softball turf projects to replace end-of-life artificial turf at A.C. Bible, Jr. Memorial Stadium, alongside the same reallocation for Monroe Stadium. Bond Oversight Committee cited safety concerns from aged turf; replacement scheduled for summer 2026 to avoid disrupting the athletic season. The district's most recent page update (May 2026) confirms turf is actively being laid out at Monroe Stadium; Bible Stadium's status is inferred from the shared line item and reported to commence later in May 2026, after Monroe -- not yet independently confirmed by a Bible-specific status sentence, hence needs_review.

leads.summary: Leander ISD's turf replacement at Ed W. Monroe Memorial Stadium (football/soccer) is actively underway as of a May 2026 status update ('the new turf is being laid out'), funded by a $1.6M Bond 2023 reallocation shared with Bible Stadium, approved by the Board Jan. 22, 2026.
leads.evidence_quote: May 2026 The new turf is being laid out at Monroe Stadium.

evidence.project_name: Monroe Stadium - turf replacement, football/soccer field
evidence.projected_start_date: Summer 2026 (turf removal/installation underway as of May 2026)
evidence.details: Leander ISD Board (Jan. 22, 2026) approved reallocating $1.6 million in 2023 bond project savings to fund artificial turf replacement at Ed W. Monroe Memorial Stadium (adjacent to Vandegrift HS), alongside the same reallocation for Bible Stadium. Bond Oversight Committee recommended reallocation after finding the existing turf had reached end of usable life and posed growing safety concerns; replacement scheduled for summer to avoid disrupting the athletic season. As of the district's May 2026 status update, crews had begun removing old turf with new turf installation underway. Field supports football and soccer.

leads.summary: Completed Leander ISD 2023 bond project: baseball/softball turf installed at five high schools (Cedar Park, Leander, Rouse, Vandegrift, Vista Ridge), awarded to Hellas Construction for $27,601,380; all five campuses confirmed complete with ribbon-cuttings Nov 2024-Mar 2025.
leads.evidence_quote: Now all high schools in Leander ISD have fields with artificial turf.

evidence.project_name: LISD High Schools - baseball/softball turf installation
evidence.details: Bond 2023 project: installation of artificial turf on baseball and softball fields at five high schools (Cedar Park HS, Leander HS, Rouse HS, Vandegrift HS, Vista Ridge HS); Glenn HS already had turf prior to this bond. Awarded to Hellas Construction; cost $27,601,380. Completion now confirmed with specific ribbon-cutting dates: Rouse HS (Nov 2024), Leander HS (Jan 2025), Cedar Park HS and Vista Ridge HS (Mar 2025), Vandegrift HS (Mar 2025). All five campuses complete; no further procurement action expected.

leads.summary: Completed Leander ISD 2023 bond project: artificial turf on the Running Brushy Middle School competition field ($1,186,500), confirmed complete as of August 2025, ready for the school year.
leads.evidence_quote: August 2025 The artificial turf project is complete and ready for the first day of school.

evidence.project_name: Running Brushy MS - competition field turf
evidence.project_address: 2303 N. Lakeline Blvd, Cedar Park, TX 78613
evidence.details: Bond 2023 project: installation of artificial turf on the competition field at Running Brushy MS, used for sub-varsity games and community rentals. Estimated project budget $1,186,500, funded by Bond 2023. Prior known lead was in-progress (Summer 2024 start); page now confirms completion as of August 2025, ready for the school year.

leads.summary: Round Rock ISD's Westwood HS Athletic Improvements Phase 1 (baseball/softball field turf, track resurfacing), funded by the $932M 2024 bond, is confirmed as RFP #14629365 on the district's procurement aggregator; the solicitation closed after its Feb 19, 2026 due date with no award status yet shown.

evidence.project_name: Westwood HS - baseball/softball turf, athletic improvements
evidence.details: 2024 bond package ($932M) funds installation of artificial turf on the baseball and softball fields at Westwood High School, plus running track resurfacing and other athletics facility renovations. Originally listed on the district's own procurement board (now broken, SRC-004); the district's Williamson County procurement aggregator (texasbids.net, SRC-008) confirms the formal solicitation number (RFP #14629365, due 02/19/26) and shows it as closed/past-due with no award status yet disclosed. Follow-up needed to confirm award.

leads.summary: Round Rock ISD's Stony Point HS Package 1 (softball/baseball field and fencing renovation, track resurfacing, new batting cages, plus roof/HVAC replacement), funded by the 2024 bond, is confirmed as RFP/bid #14492704 on the district's procurement aggregator; due 11/25/25, now past due (closed) with no award status shown.
leads.evidence_quote: Renovations of softball and baseball field and fencing, renovations of track surfacing, addition of new pemb batting cages, replacement of roof and curbs, replacement of hvac units to include plumbing, controls and electrical connections and renovati...

evidence.project_name: Stony Point HS - softball/baseball field renovation Package 1
evidence.details: Active bid 'Stony Point HS - Package 1' for renovation of softball and baseball fields and fencing, track surfacing renovation, new PEMB batting cage addition. Originally listed on the district's own e-bid page (now broken, SRC-004); the district's Williamson County procurement aggregator (texasbids.net, SRC-008) confirms the formal bid number (#14492704, due 11/25/25), shows it closed/past-due with no award disclosed, and adds roof/curb and HVAC replacement scope not previously recorded. Turf-material specifics for the softball/baseball fields still require validation.
